# Contract: Coverage Classifier FFI (UniFFI Swift boundary)

**Feature**: `014-coverage` | **Date**: 2026-07-19
**Module**: `kaname-core` (Rust) → `KanameCore` (Swift, generated by UniFFI)

Additive to the P1 + all-reader + balance-chain + reconciliation + cross-source-dedup `engine-ffi` contracts. The
"external interface" is the exported UniFFI surface (not an HTTP API); generated Swift signatures are the
consumer-facing form. This slice adds **one function** (`compute_coverage`) and **four new types** (`CoverageState`
enum + `StatementCoverage` / `TransactionCoverage` / `MonthCoverage` records). It **reuses** the existing
`NaiveDate` custom type (ISO-8601 `String`) **unchanged**, requires **no `uniffi.toml` change**, and uses **no
money type** (no `Decimal`). Mirrors the `cross_source_duplicates`-wraps-`dedup::cross_source_duplicates`
precedent exactly, including the name-clash handling.

---

## Exported function

### `compute_coverage`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn compute_coverage(today: NaiveDate, statements: Vec<StatementCoverage>, transactions: Vec<TransactionCoverage>) -> Vec<MonthCoverage>` |
| Swift | `public func computeCoverage(today: String, statements: [StatementCoverage], transactions: [TransactionCoverage]) -> [MonthCoverage]` |

**Contract**

- Pure classifier over a `today` date + **two pre-aggregated fact lists** (supplied by the platform, which owns
  aggregation/persistence), scoped to **one account**. **Never** reads the network/**clock**/locale/global state,
  never opens a file/PDF, never queries a database (FR-003/011/013/014).
- Returns **exactly 24** `MonthCoverage` entries, **oldest first**, for the rolling 24 months ending at `today`'s
  calendar month — for **any** input, including empty lists (⇒ 24 GAP/false) (FR-001, SC-001/007).
- **Classification** (per month): **COVERED** iff a statement's `periodEnd` falls in it **or** a transaction in it
  has `fromFullStatement == true`; else **PARTIAL** iff it has any transaction; else **GAP**. `needsReview` is
  true **only** on a COVERED month backed by a needs-review statement (OR over that month's statements); PARTIAL/
  GAP and COVERED-via-transaction-only are `false` (FR-005/006/007).
- **Purity / totality**: identical input ⇒ identical output; never panics; no failure is surfaced to Swift (total
  function) (FR-003, SC-006/012). **No money** is computed — no `Decimal`, no `f64` (spec Assumptions).

**Consumer expectations (app/test)**

- Pass **`today`** explicitly (the platform's current date as an ISO-8601 `String`, e.g. `"2026-06-14"`) — the
  engine never reads the clock (FR-003).
- Index the 24 results by `month` (a `[String: MonthCoverage]`) to look up a specific month, or render them in
  order (already oldest-first). Treat `state` (`.gap`/`.partial`/`.covered`) as the coverage signal and
  `needsReview` as the trust qualifier on COVERED months.
- The platform supplies the facts scoped to one account (this call does no cross-account attribution) (FR-013).

> Internally: the exported `compute_coverage` wraps `crate::coverage::compute_coverage(today, &statements,
> &transactions)` — passing `today` **by value** (`NaiveDate: Copy`) and the owned `Vec`s as **borrowed** slices,
> exactly as `cross_source_duplicates` wraps `dedup::cross_source_duplicates(&existing, &incoming)`. Because the
> FFI wrapper name `compute_coverage` **shadows** the pure function, `ffi.rs` imports only the coverage **types**
> (not the pure fn) and calls it fully-qualified. `lib.rs` re-exports the **FFI** function
> (`pub use ffi::compute_coverage;`) and the coverage types + window helper
> (`pub use coverage::{CoverageState, MonthCoverage, StatementCoverage, TransactionCoverage, month_window};`). The
> pure `coverage::compute_coverage` is **not** re-exported at the crate root (name clash) — `tests/parity.rs` uses
> the FFI-exported one via `kaname_core::compute_coverage`.

---

## Types

### `CoverageState` (NEW) — `uniffi::Enum`

| Rust | Swift |
|-|-|
| `enum CoverageState { Gap, Partial, Covered }` | `.gap` / `.partial` / `.covered` |

Three variants. Derives `Debug/Clone/Copy/PartialEq/Eq/Serialize/Deserialize/uniffi::Enum` (serde is a fixture
convenience; mirrors `Direction`/`DedupLayer` plus serde).

### `StatementCoverage` (NEW) — `uniffi::Record` (input)

```swift
public struct StatementCoverage {
    public var periodEnd: String     // ISO-8601 date, e.g. "2026-05-16"
    public var needsReview: Bool     // the run was incomplete / failed reconciliation
}
```

### `TransactionCoverage` (NEW) — `uniffi::Record` (input)

```swift
public struct TransactionCoverage {
    public var date: String              // ISO-8601 date, e.g. "2026-04-10"
    public var fromFullStatement: Bool   // came from a full statement (vs a live alert)
}
```

### `MonthCoverage` (NEW) — `uniffi::Record` (output)

```swift
public struct MonthCoverage {
    public var month: String            // "YYYY-MM"
    public var state: CoverageState     // .gap / .partial / .covered
    public var needsReview: Bool        // true only on a COVERED month backed by a needs-review statement
}
```

Records derive `Debug/Clone/PartialEq/uniffi::Record` (`MonthCoverage` + `CoverageState` also derive serde so the
Rust parity harness can deserialize expected entries — no effect on the Swift surface). `NaiveDate` fields cross as
ISO-8601 `String`s via the existing custom type; field/case names are lower-camel-cased by UniFFI.

### `NaiveDate` (REUSED — unchanged)

- The `today` parameter and the record date fields cross as an ISO-8601 (`%Y-%m-%d`) `String` via the existing
  `uniffi::custom_type!(NaiveDate, String, …)` in `ffi.rs`. **No new custom type, no `uniffi.toml` change.** No
  `Decimal`/money type is used by this slice.

---

## Contract tests (test-first — FR-020)

**Rust (`cargo test`)** — engine-side truth (`tests/parity.rs` + `coverage.rs` unit tests):

- `coverage_map_matches_expected` (parity): load `fixtures/coverage/basic.json`, build the typed inputs (parse ISO
  dates), call `compute_coverage`, and assert the returned `Vec<MonthCoverage>` equals the 24 `expected_months`
  (SC-003/008).
- `coverage.rs` unit tests additionally cover `month_window` (24 labels / endpoints / determinism), the reference
  scenario, empty input, out-of-window/future facts, and COVERED-via-full-txn-only `needsReview` (SC-001..SC-007).

**Swift (Swift Testing, `import KanameCore`, `ios/Tests/CoverageTests.swift`)** — "core ↔ Swift coverage map"
(mirrors `ReconcileTests`/`CrossSourceDedupTests`):

- Build `[StatementCoverage]` + `[TransactionCoverage]` for the reference scenario
  (`StatementCoverage(periodEnd: "2026-05-16", needsReview: false)`, `TransactionCoverage(date: "2026-04-10",
  fromFullStatement: false)`, …) and call `computeCoverage(today: "2026-06-14", statements:, transactions:)`.
- Assert `count == 24`; index by `month` and assert `2026-05` `.covered`/false, `2026-02` `.covered`/true,
  `2026-04` `.partial`, `2026-01` `.covered`/false, and a sample GAP month `.gap` (SC-009).
- `CoverageState` surfaces as `.gap`/`.partial`/`.covered`; records as `periodEnd`/`needsReview` /
  `date`/`fromFullStatement` / `month`/`state`/`needsReview`. Any comment sits on its **own line above** the code
  (swift-format `[Spacing]` forbids trailing inline `//`).

---

## Stability / compatibility

- Adding one function + four types is additive; no existing signature changes, and `NaiveDate` is reused
  unchanged, so existing callers, fixtures, and the generated Swift for prior slices are unaffected.
- The generated `Generated/kaname_core.swift` + `KanameCoreFFI.xcframework` are build artifacts, rebuilt by
  `make core-xcframework` (which runs **before** `tuist generate`). **This contract**, not the generated files, is
  the reviewed source of truth.
